news_items.title: Calf injury: Muheim misses Swiss World Cup match against Bosnia - Newswav
news_items.text:
Calf injury: Muheim misses Swiss World Cup match against Bosnia - Newswav

Back

# Calf injury: Muheim misses Swiss World Cup match against Bosnia

Football Sports

18 Jun 2026 • 4:51 AM MYT

DPA International DPA, founded in 1949, one of the world’s leading independent news agencies

FILE PHOTO - Switzerland's Miro Muheim (L) and Germany's Leroy Sane battle for the ball during the International friendly soccer match between Switzerland and Germany at St. Jakob-Park. Christian Charisius/dpa

Switzerland will be without defender Miro Muheim for their second World Cup match on Thursday against Bosnia-Herzegovina for injury reasons.

The Swiss FA said that the SV Hamburg player is nursing a calf muscle problem and can't train with the team.

The Swiss were held 1-1 by Qatar in their opening match. Muheim came on as a late substitute and headed an own goal in stoppage time.

Continue reading in app View Original Article

4 ViewsThe Independent

# Switzerland Predicted Lineup vs Bosnia and Herzegovina at the 2026 FIFA World Cup

## The Nati will look to bounce back from their draw against Qatar, while the Bosnians also aim for their first win after sharing the points with Canada.

Switzerland endured a frustrating start to the 2026 FIFA World Cup after Qatar secured their first-ever World Cup point, snatching a last-minute equalizer in Santa Clara.

The Nati now turn their attention to Thursday, June 18, when they face Bosnia and Herzegovina—another European side that also let victory slip against co-host Canada in Group B. With that in mind
